name: LoanMax Title Loans
description_short: LoanMax provides auto title loans in Michigan and nationwide, allowing borrowers to access cash up to $10,000 by using their vehicle's equity as collateral.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over 25 years as a title lending company focused on helping working Americans access cash using their vehicle equity. The company operates multiple locations across Michigan, including the Southgate branch at 12850 Eureka Road, and maintains a nationwide presence with state-specific licensing and compliance frameworks.

LoanMax's core offering is auto title loans, which allow borrowers to borrow up to $10,000 by pledging their vehicle title as collateral. The application process requires only a vehicle, valid photo ID, and a clear vehicle title. The Southgate location accepts multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union. The company operates with consistent hours (Monday-Friday 10 AM-6 PM, Saturday 9 AM-2 PM) and maintains nearby locations in Taylor, Garden City, Detroit, and Center Line for customer convenience.

LoanMax distinguishes itself through established operations (33+ years in business as of 2023), a multi-state licensing infrastructure with documented compliance in Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, Nevada, and Michigan, and a network of physical locations rather than online-only lending. The company provides regulatory transparency by publishing state-specific consumer information, maximum rate schedules, and complaint procedures for each regulated market.

As a title lending company, LoanMax serves borrowers facing immediate cash needs but carries inherent risks typical of secured lending against vehicles. Borrowers should understand that title loans are short-term solutions with vehicle seizure as a default consequence, and the website includes Nevada-specific warnings about using title loans only for short-term needs. The lack of transparent APR/interest rate information on the public-facing website is a notable limitation for prospective borrowers evaluating true cost of borrowing.
pros: Fast cash access up to $10,000 using vehicle equity as collateral
Established 33+ year company history with multi-state presence and physical locations
Simple application requirements: only vehicle, photo ID, and clear title needed
Multiple payment methods accepted including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union
State-specific compliance documentation available for Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, Nevada, and Michigan
Convenient hours including Saturday availability (9 AM-2 PM)
Multiple nearby locations in the Detroit metro area for customer access
cons: No transparent APR or interest rate information displayed on website, making true cost of borrowing opaque
Vehicle seizure risk upon default—title is held as collateral with repossession as enforcement mechanism
Nevada disclaimer explicitly warns title loans should only be used for short-term needs, not long-term solutions
Limited information on loan terms, repayment periods, or default consequences on public website
No online application or funding capability mentioned—requires in-person store visit
best_for: Borrowers with immediate cash needs ($100-$10,000) who own a vehicle with clear title and cannot access traditional credit
Individuals in the Detroit metropolitan area seeking same-day or next-day emergency funding
People needing short-term bridge loans while awaiting other income or facing unexpected expenses
diagnosis: LoanMax Title Loans is best for working individuals with immediate cash needs ($1K-$10K) who own a vehicle outright and lack access to traditional credit or faster alternatives. The critical caveat is that title loans carry high risk due to vehicle seizure consequences upon default and typically involve substantial interest rates—borrowers should only use this product for genuine short-term emergencies and should explore payday alternatives or credit counseling before committing.
services: Vehicle evaluation and loan approval process
In-person loan application at physical store locations
Multi-location network access across Michigan and nationwide
State-specific compliance documentation and consumer information
Customer support via phone (877-511-CASH national line, 734-237-7887 Southgate location)
